overview: Sultai Flow Doomsday is a combo deck that aims to cast Doomsday as early as possible to stack cycling cards and win on the following turn with Thassa's Oracle. The inclusion of Flow State provides crucial resilience against discard and counterspell-heavy metas, allowing the pilot to recover key pieces or hoard resources. Green mana adds utility through Veil of Summer, Carpet of Flowers, and Witherbloom Charm to navigate hate cards.
win_conditions: Barrowgoyf and Dauthi Voidwalker midrange plan

keep_criteria: Hands that can find the combo through cantrips
Hands with protection, card draw, and Doomsday
Hands that can activate Flow State early or win on turn 2 with backup
Keep hands containing Doomsday plus two or more enablers/tutors (Tamiyo, Flow State, Thoughtseize, Duress) and two or more basic lands.
Keep hands with three or more enablers/tutors and two lands if you can reliably find Doomsday.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with lots of disruption but only a few cantrips
Mulligan hands with fewer than two lands, Doomsday with zero enablers, or hands with only enablers and no reliable way to fetch Doomsday.

examples.reason: Has protection, card draw, and Doomsday, providing everything needed to set up the combo.
Nearly unusable on its own but can find what's needed and activate Flow State as early as turn 2.

early_game: Play cantrips like Brainstorm and Ponder to find Doomsday and key combo pieces.
Use Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal for early mana acceleration.
Cast Doomsday as soon as possible to stack cycling cards.
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Thoughtseize or Duress to remove opponent's combo pieces, counterspells, or ramp sources.
Turn 2: Play a land, cast Flow State or Tamiyo to tutor for Doomsday or additional enablers.
Sequence mana to ensure you can disrupt on turn 1 and assemble on turn 2 or 3.
If opening with multiple lands, consider holding a land drop to guarantee you can cast Doomsday when ready.
mid_game: Execute the combo by playing Thassa's Oracle with 2 or fewer cards in the deck.
Use Force of Will, Daze, and Veil of Summer to protect the combo from disruption.
Stack cards like Cavern of Souls or Thoughtseize to bypass enemy counters or hate cards.
Conserve mana for Doomsday's five mana cost and protect your hand size, as Doomsday requires exactly five cards.
Use Tamiyo to mill or search efficiently, and leverage Flow State's graveyard recursion to tutor without losing card advantage.
Avoid drawing extra cards once you have the full five-card combo assembled.
Hold back on committing threats until you have the complete five-card hand.
Use disruption to clear the path for Doomsday.
If the opponent has a clear win condition, prioritize disruption over combo assembly.
late_game: Use Flow State to recover from discards or unexpected counterspells.
If the combo is stalled, hoard resources with Flow State or transition to a midrange plan with Barrowgoyf and Dauthi Voidwalker post-sideboard.
Doomsday plus four other cards in your graveyard and one in hand.
Common UB/G finishers include Mindslaver, Time Spiral, or Empty Library setups.
Once Doomsday resolves, arrange the four graveyard cards on top of your library to win instantly or set up an unbeatable state, then play the top card if possible.
When you have exactly five cards in hand, including Doomsday and four enablers/wincons.
When the opponent has no clear answer to Doomsday or has already used their interaction.
When you can protect the combo with disruption, counters, or graveyard protection.

key_cards.name: Doomsday
key_cards.notes: The core spell that allows you to stack the top 4 cards of your deck to set up the win condition.

key_cards.name: Thassa's Oracle
key_cards.notes: Wins the game when played with 2 or fewer cards in your deck.

key_cards.name: Flow State
key_cards.notes: Allows you to recover key cards after discards or counters, and hoard resources against passive opponents.

key_cards.name: Brainstorm
key_cards.notes: Essential for finding Doomsday and combo pieces early in the game.

key_cards.name: Force of Will
key_cards.notes: Key counterspell to protect the combo, often used as backup for turn-2 wins.

key_cards.name: Witherbloom Charm
key_cards.notes: Flexible answer against Chalice of the Void and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben; can give life or draw cards.

key_cards.name: Veil of Summer
key_cards.notes: Counterspell that also protects against discard spells.

key_cards.name: Carpet of Flowers
key_cards.notes: Creates an absurd amount of mana to hoard resources and push through defenses.

key_cards.name: Barrowgoyf
key_cards.notes: Transforms the deck into a midrange strategy post-sideboard, particularly effective against aggressive decks.

key_cards.name: Jace, Wielder of Mysteries
key_cards.notes: Replaces Thassa's Oracle when met with Surgical Extraction and is immune to Consign to Memory.

sequencing_tips: Stack cycling cards like Edge of Autumn and Street Wraith on top of the deck to draw Thassa's Oracle.
Stack Cavern of Souls to bypass counterspells or Chalice of the Void for 2.
Stack Thoughtseize to remove enemy Consign to Memory or Mindbreak Trap.
Stack Lion's Eye Diamond to generate blue mana and play around Blood Moon.
Put a land or blue card on top to pay for Force of Will while playing Thoughtseize on turn 2.
Doomsday requires exactly five cards in hand
drawing extra cards before casting it will break the combo.
Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student can mill or search, but be careful not to mill Doomsday into your graveyard unless you have a way to retrieve it.
Flow State can be cast from the graveyard, making it resilient to discard.
Common mistakes include overcommitting to the board, drawing too many cards before casting Doomsday, and failing to track opponent's hand size.
Always prioritize disruption and hand integrity over tempo.

matchups.advice: Flow State is critical to recover lost cards. Do not go for the aggro game plan as they will out-Dimir you.
Sideboard in Carpet of Flowers as a giant threat while fighting for resources. Use Veil of Summer in main.
Play the combo fast. Sideboard in Barrowgoyf to force them to keep dead removal cards, buying time.
Use Force of Will, discards, and Veil of Summer to stop their combo. Force of Negation can stop Dark Ritual as a turn-0 answer.
Watch Trinisphere's effect on your combo turn. Use Witherbloom Charm to hit Grim Monolith for time. Sideboard in more answers.

Use Force of Will, Flash, or split your combo assembly to avoid single-target counters. Cast Doomsday in response to their counterspells if you have protection. Use Tamiyo and Flow State to set up multiple threats or win conditions that are harder to counter, and prioritize disrupting their hand early.

Disrupt their mana base early with Thoughtseize and Duress. Use Force of Will to protect your combo. If they get ahead, use Tamiyo to mill or search for a faster wincon. Bring in sideboard hate and artifact/enchantment removal to slow their engine.

Prioritize early disruption to remove key threats. Use Force of Will to protect your combo. If you can't stabilize, concede early. Tamiyo can mill to find Doomsday faster, but only if you can survive the initial turns.

Out-value them with card advantage (Tamiyo, Flow State). Use disruption to remove their key answers. Set up a win condition that is hard to interact with, and avoid overcommitting to the board. Force them to spend resources on your combo while you assemble it.

Prioritize disruption to remove their Doomsday or enablers. Use Force of Will to protect your combo. Track their graveyard and hand size. If they have Doomsday, use Surgical Extraction or Rest in Peace. Win by going first, having better disruption, or out-resolving their Doomsday setup.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells (Force of Will, Counterspell), graveyard hate (Rest in Peace, Leyline of the Void), and fast combo threats.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: The secret behind this list is playing the sorcery that names it ( Doomsday !) as soon as possible and winning in the following turn with Thassa's Oracle.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: winning in the following turn with Thassa's Oracle
Jace, Wielder of Mysteries . It is an alternative win condition that replaces Thassa's Oracle
with 4 Barrowgoyf s and 2 Dauthi Voidwalker s, you'll turn this list into a sort of Dimir Midrange

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: You'll usually do this by stacking cards with cycling, like Edge of Autumn and Street Wraith , on top of your deck and playing Oracle with 2 or less cards in your deck.
For instance, you can stack Cavern of Souls to go over counterspells or Chalice of the Void for 2, or stack Thoughtseize to get Consign to Memory or Mindbreak Trap out of your way.
You can also stack Lion's Eye Diamond to play around Blood Moon .

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Veil of Summer is amazing against counters and discards.
Witherbloom Charm , one of the new cards, is a flexible answer against several problematic cards, like Chalice of the Void and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben
stack Lion's Eye Diamond to play around Blood Moon
Consign to Memory is still the most popular card for Legacy sideboards
Karn, the Great Creator disables your mana artifacts.
